concerns.text: The LaTeX source `main-llmxive.tex` is a monolithic 1000+ line file generated by a script, mixing preamble, shim layers, and full body content. This violates modularity principles and risks hitting the 32K token output limit during future edits. Split into `paper/source/preamble.tex` (packages/metadata), `paper/source/shims.tex` (macro overrides), and `paper/source/body.tex` (content), then include via `\input` in a minimal `main.tex`.

The file contains hardcoded Chinese comments (e.g., `%不需要目录就注释掉...`) in `main-llmxive.tex` and `paper.tex`. These are artifacts of the generation process and should be removed or translated to English to maintain code quality and international readability standards.

The `paper/source/sections/` directory contains 7 separate `.tex` files (e.g., `1_introduction.tex`, `7_appendix.tex`), but `main-llmxive.tex` appears to be a flattened concatenation of these. Ensure the build pipeline consistently uses `\input` for modularity rather than generating a single massive file, which hinders version control diffs and targeted editing.
